title: hephaestus-character-sheet-lame-2k
prompt:
A character model sheet / animation turntable, on a clean plain white studio background.

SUBJECT — extract and render ONLY the bearded, fair-haired smith on the LEFT of the reference image. Ignore the gold automata and the industrial background entirely. He is HEPHAESTUS: a MIDDLE-AGED smith (not an old man, not white-haired), broad and stooped in build, fair/blond shoulder-length hair, a full beard, a weary tender watchful expression. He wears a pale cream work-robe draped off one shoulder. Keep his exact face, hair, beard, build and costume identical across every view.

HIS LAMENESS IS A DEFINING TRAIT AND MUST READ CLEARLY: his right leg is visibly lamed — the lower leg twisted and the foot turned inward (a club foot), the calf wasted/thinner than the sound left leg. He cannot bear weight evenly: he stoops, hip dropped, weight thrown onto the sound leg, and leans on a simple worn wooden staff/crutch for support. This asymmetry must be obvious in every full-body view.

LAYOUT — a multi-angle turntable spread, evenly and identically lit across all views:
- full-body FRONT view
- full-body THREE-QUARTER FRONT view
- full-body BACK view
plus two clearly labeled detail callout panels: a head-and-shoulders FACE close-up, and a LEG DETAIL close-up of the lamed, twisted lower leg and turned foot.

STYLE — painterly cinematic concept-art rendering matching the reference image's medium and palette (warm gold skin tones against cool light, rich brushwork). NOT photoreal, NOT a flat line drawing.

Clean white background, no environment, no other characters, no props beyond his costume and his support staff, only faint angle markers. 16:9 aspect ratio.
